title: The AI Boom Has a Second Act—And It’s Playing Out in Optics
author: Bridget Bennett
description: While AI infrastructure investment focuses on chips, the real opportunity lies in optical networking components. Companies manufacturing fiber, connectors, and routing systems are landing multi-billion-dollar contracts with hyperscalers and facing supply bottlenecks. Three key players—Amphenol, Corning, and Ciena—are positioned to benefit from the $1 trillion AI infrastructure buildout, with analyst estimates expected to be revised higher as demand accelerates through 2030.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong recent earnings beat with revenue of $7.62B vs. $7.08B estimate, double-digit growth projections through 2028, and positioned as core supplier of connectors/interconnects for AI data centers with $200B market cap.

Secured major deals with NVIDIA and Amazon for optical fiber manufacturing, projecting sales growth from current levels to $20B by year-end, $30B by 2028, and $40B by 2030, with EPS accelerating significantly through 2028.

Recent earnings beat with EPS of $1.64 vs. $1.46 estimate, raised full-year guidance to $6.3B, margin expansion expected, and positioned as intelligent routing layer with strong growth projections to $14.28 EPS by 2028.

title: Coaxial Cable Market Size to Hit USD 46.24 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global coaxial cable market, valued at $21.58 billion in 2025, is expected to grow to $46.24 billion by 2035 at a 7.94% CAGR. Growth is driven by rising broadband demand, high-speed internet expansion, and 5G deployment. North America leads with 34.8% market share, while Asia Pacific is expected to grow fastest at 10.12% CAGR. Key segments include video distribution (48.5% share) and internet data transfer (fastest growing at 10.89% CAGR).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a leading market player but no specific recent developments or initiatives mentioned in the article.
Listed as a leading market player but no specific recent developments or initiatives mentioned in the article.

Company expanded global production capabilities in 2026 to support offshore wind HVDC interconnections and digital grid modernization, indicating strong market positioning and growth initiatives in the expanding coaxial cable and broadband infrastructure sector.

title: Cable Assembly Market Size to Hit $348.66 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global cable assembly market is projected to grow from USD 194.54 billion in 2025 to USD 348.66 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 6.03%. Growth is driven by rising demand for high-speed connectivity, 5G networks, cloud computing, and digital infrastructure expansion. Custom cable assemblies and fiber-optic assemblies lead in market segments, while Asia Pacific dominates regionally with 38.46% market share.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a leading market player in the growing cable assembly market, positioned to benefit from expanding demand for high-speed connectivity and digital infrastructure across multiple industries.
Leading market player with recent product innovation (2025: Ultra Low-Profile PCIe Gen 7 connectors for AI and data center applications), demonstrating strong positioning in high-growth segments.
Listed as leading market player, well-positioned to benefit from automotive electrification and transportation connectivity trends driving significant market growth.
Leading market player positioned to benefit from high-growth fiber-optic segment expanding at 11.40% CAGR and increasing broadband infrastructure deployment.

title: 3 Great Value AI Stocks (Hint: 1 is a Household Name; the Other 2 You've Never Heard Of)
author: Lee Samaha
description: The article identifies three AI stocks offering good value: Alphabet (Google), which is investing heavily in AI infrastructure with potential for strong future free cash flow; Brady Corporation, benefiting from rapid data center-related wire identification product growth and the upcoming Honeywell PSS acquisition; and Belden, positioned to benefit from both data center buildout through 2030 and subsequent inference spending growth, with the RUCKUS Networks acquisition expanding its enterprise networking exposure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong AI infrastructure investment positioning with projected steady 30% FCF margins and $1 trillion+ revenue by 2030s, potentially reaching $6.7 trillion valuation. Capital spending expected to moderate as data center buildout completes.

Strong AI infrastructure investment positioning with projected steady 30% FCF margins and $1 trillion+ revenue by 2030s, potentially reaching $6.7 trillion valuation. Capital spending expected to moderate as data center buildout completes.

Data center wire identification products growing 19% and 13% across regions, significantly outpacing overall company growth. Honeywell PSS acquisition is transformative, combining mobile scanning expertise with labeling capabilities. Trading at attractive 14.4x 2027 earnings.

Well-positioned for both data center buildout through 2030 and inference spending growth thereafter. RUCKUS Networks acquisition increases enterprise networking exposure. Trading at significant discount to peers like TE Connectivity and Amphenol despite double-digit data center exposure growth.
Mentioned as a peer comparison point; trades at premium valuation relative to Belden despite similar data center exposure, suggesting less attractive value proposition.

title: Nasdaq 100 Hits Record Above 30,000 On Micron's 18% Rally: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: The Nasdaq 100 crossed 30,000 during intraday trading as semiconductor stocks surged, led by Micron Technology's 18% gain following a UBS analyst price target hike to $1,625. The rally was driven by increased AI spending expectations from hyperscalers, with chip stocks dominating market leadership. The S&P 500 rose 0.6%, while the Russell 2000 outperformed with a 1.7% gain. AutoZone plunged 10.1% after missing revenue estimates despite beating EPS.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 18% on UBS analyst price target hike to $1,625 (from $535), driven by strong AI memory demand expectations. Market cap exceeded $1 trillion for the first time, with stock up 200% year-to-date.
Jumped 12.3% tracking the broader semiconductor rally and benefiting from announced cash dividend, with no company-specific negative catalyst.
Rose 9.1% on sympathy buying with the semiconductor sector rotation, driven by positive AI infrastructure spending sentiment.
Climbed 8.7% lifted by the chip sector rotation and increased AI-related spending expectations.
Added 8.4% as part of the broader semiconductor and tech sector rally driven by AI infrastructure demand.
Gained 6.8% as cheaper jet fuel and Iran deal narrative boosted airline stocks, lifting the broader airline sector.
Plunged 10.1% despite beating EPS estimates, due to revenue miss ($4.84B vs $4.88B estimate) and soft domestic same-store sales of 4.1% against elevated expectations.
Dropped 6% as investors trimmed positions ahead of May 28 earnings announcement.
